item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this is a hidden gem.

item.title: Re: I don't think this is a necropost, as long as the scp is still up, you can still talk about it
item.content:encoded: This is definitely a necropost, as well and contributing nothing to the discussion.

item.title: I don't think this is a necropost, as long as the scp is still up, you can still talk about it
item.content:encoded: (although i suppose This comment im making is

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: little bit of humor mixed in with the professional style of the form, I like it alot. The small details really bring it to life (the movie/snuff preference, small jokes in the quotes.) Some might argue it takes away the professional style of writing but I think you maintain a balance. The one things is just some grammar here and there but still an upvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Subtle, I like it. The cross-reference to the Witch Girl is especially delicious. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Easier to a certain degree, I guess, but using up more resources. And if he suddenly gets some complication, he might die on them anyways. And if his effect is not active when he's healed, they could either Class A him and let him go, make him part of the Foundation, or try to recreate the conditions allowing him to gain telepathy. The latter seems more likely of the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because he's easier to contain in this state, and it might even lead to item loss. God knows if his effect is still active while he's not comatose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why don't they just give the poor guy Panacea? Then they wouldn't have to spend as much keeping him alive. Then he could be contained like any other humanoid SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This manages to combine creepy and funny very well while staying professional. It's a simple idea, but very well constructed. Would like to see more experiments results, in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this is a necropost, but SCP-041 should not have a nasogastric tube. They are designed for short-term feedings of only a couple of weeks, since they cause erosion of the skin and nose. The correct term is a gastrostomy tube, which goes through the abdomen into the stomach, and can stay in place for years. In addition a pacemaker is not needed even if his nervous system is not working. After all, transplanted hearts work just fine without any neurological connections.
Otherwise, I really like this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Joke reference needs to be gone. Also, there's a few small bits of writing that might need touching up. Good other than that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, this references a -J article in a normal list SCP entry? Is that allowed?
